# Data Cleansing Component

Fix common data quality issues in string columns ‚Äî nulls, whitespace, case normalization, and punctuation removal. Operates on all string columns or a configurable subset.

This component uses Dagster's IO manager to pass DataFrames between assets. No IO manager configuration is required for local development ‚Äî Dagster's default FilesystemIOManager handles serialization automatically.

<!-- FIELDS:START - auto-generated by tools/regen_readme_fields.py -->

## Fields

### Required

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `asset_name` | `str` | Output Dagster asset name |
| `upstream_asset_key` | `str` | Upstream asset key providing a DataFrame |

### Catalog metadata

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `group_name` | `str` | ‚Äî | Dagster asset group name |
| `owners` | `List[str]` | ‚Äî | Asset owners ‚Äî list of team names or email addresses, e.g. ['team:analytics', 'user@company.com'] |
| `asset_tags` | `Dict[str, str]` | ‚Äî | Additional key-value tags to apply to the asset, e.g. {'domain': 'finance', 'tier': 'gold'} |
| `kinds` | `List[str]` | ‚Äî | Asset kinds for the Dagster catalog, e.g. ['snowflake', 'python']. Auto-inferred from component name if not set. |
| `column_lineage` | `Dict[str, List[str]]` | ‚Äî | Column-level lineage mapping: output column name ‚Üí list of upstream column names it was derived from, e.g. {'revenue': ['price', 'quantity']} |
| `description` | `str` | ‚Äî | Asset description shown in the Dagster catalog. |
| `deps` | `List[str]` | ‚Äî | Lineage-only upstream asset keys (no data passed at runtime). |

### Freshness

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `freshness_max_lag_minutes` | `int` | ‚Äî | Maximum acceptable lag in minutes before the asset is considered stale. Defines a FreshnessPolicy. |
| `freshness_cron` | `str` | ‚Äî | Cron schedule string for the freshness policy, e.g. '0 9 * * 1-5' (weekdays at 9am). |

### Partitions

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `partition_type` | `str` | ‚Äî | Partition type: 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly', 'hourly', 'static', 'multi', or None for unpartitioned |
| `partition_start` | `str` | ‚Äî | Partition start date in ISO format, e.g. '2024-01-01'. Required for time-based partition types. |
| `partition_date_column` | `str` | ‚Äî | Column used to filter upstream DataFrame to the current date partition key. |
| `partition_dimensions` | `List[Dict[str, Any]]` | ‚Äî | Multi-axis partition spec: list of {name, type, start, values, dynamic_partition_name} dicts. Overrides flat fields when set. |
| `partition_values` | `str` | ‚Äî | Comma-separated values for static or multi partitioning, e.g. 'customer_a,customer_b,customer_c'. |
| `partition_static_dim` | `str` | ‚Äî | Dimension name for the static axis in multi-partitioning, e.g. 'customer' or 'region'. |
| `partition_static_column` | `str` | ‚Äî | Column used to filter upstream DataFrame to the current static partition dimension (e.g. 'customer_id'). |

### Retry policy

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `retry_policy_max_retries` | `int` | ‚Äî | Max retries on failure. Defines a RetryPolicy when set. |
| `retry_policy_delay_seconds` | `int` | ‚Äî | Seconds between retries (default 1). |
| `retry_policy_backoff` | `str` | `"exponential"` | Backoff strategy: 'linear' or 'exponential'. |

### Other

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `null_handling` | `str` | `"keep"` | How to handle nulls: 'keep', 'drop' (drop rows with any null), or 'fill' (fill with null_fill_value) |
| `null_fill_value` | `str` | ‚Äî | Value to fill nulls with when null_handling='fill' |
| `trim_whitespace` | `bool` | `true` | Strip leading/trailing whitespace from string columns |
| `normalize_case` | `str` | ‚Äî | Case normalization: None, 'upper', 'lower', or 'title' |
| `remove_punctuation` | `bool` | `false` | Remove punctuation characters from string columns |
| `columns` | `List[str]` | ‚Äî | Apply transformations only to these columns (None = all string columns) |
| `dynamic_partition_name` | `str` | ‚Äî | Name for DynamicPartitionsDefinition (when partition_type='dynamic'), e.g. 'tenants'. |
| `include_preview_metadata` | `bool` | `false` | Include a preview of the output data in metadata (first 5 rows as a markdown table). Used by builder UIs to render asset shape without warehouse access. |
| `preview_rows` | `int` | `25` | Rows to include in the preview metadata when `include_preview_metadata` is True. For long DataFrames (>10x preview_rows), a random sample is used so the preview reflects the data distribution; otherwise head() is used. |

<!-- FIELDS:END -->

## Example YAML

```yaml
type: dagster_component_templates.DataCleansingComponent
attributes:
  asset_name: cleansed_customers
  upstream_asset_key: raw_customers
  null_handling: fill
  null_fill_value: "unknown"
  trim_whitespace: true
  normalize_case: lower
  remove_punctuation: false
  columns:
    - first_name
    - last_name
    - email
    - city
  group_name: data_quality
```

## Null Handling Modes

- **`keep`** ‚Äî Leave null values unchanged (default).
- **`drop`** ‚Äî Drop any row that contains at least one null value.
- **`fill`** ‚Äî Replace all null values with `null_fill_value`.

## Case Normalization

When `normalize_case` is set, the specified transformation is applied to every targeted string column after whitespace trimming:

- `"upper"` ‚Äî Convert to ALL CAPS.
- `"lower"` ‚Äî Convert to all lowercase.
- `"title"` ‚Äî Capitalize the first letter of each word.

## Punctuation Removal

When `remove_punctuation: true`, all characters in Python's `string.punctuation` set are removed from each targeted string column. This includes: `!"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^_`{|}~`.

## Column Targeting

By default, all pandas `object`-dtype columns are targeted. Use `columns` to restrict processing to a named subset. Columns listed in `columns` that are not present in the DataFrame are skipped with a warning.

## Notes

- Null handling is applied before string transformations, so `fillna` values are also subject to trimming and case normalization.
- The component operates on a copy of the upstream DataFrame ‚Äî the original asset is never mutated.
